status_at_import: 4 articles and 7 tools merged via text-cleanup-enhancements modules.

user_scenarios: A student has a 1,000-word limit and wants to know whether the conclusion needs cuts.
A writer wants a six-minute read badge and needs a rough word target.
A presenter wants to know if a script will fit a five-minute slot.
A marketer needs a shorter meta description or platform caption.
tips: Check word count before and after edits to confirm the draft moved toward the target.
Use characters without spaces only when the requirement asks for that specific count.
If average words per sentence is high, scan for long sentences that could be split.
Use the Reading Time Calculator when adjustable WPM is more important than general stats.
Use the Syllable Counter after length checks when rhythm or line timing matters.

internal_links.reason: Use when the user cares about letters, vowels, consonants, or frequency.
Use when timing is the primary task.
Useful when count checks are part of a cleanup workflow.

internal_links.anchor_text: word count vs character count guide
internal_links.reason: Explains which metric fits each requirement.

schema_suggestions.notes: Describe the browser-based utility, input/output, and free access without implying server processing.
Use the enhanced FAQ questions and answers.
Use concise how-to steps when the page template supports it.
Match the Word Tools or Calculators cluster path.

existing_features: Counts words, characters, no-space characters, sentences, paragraphs, and time estimates.
Shows average word length and average words per sentence.
Surfaces a readability hint when sentences are long.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add target limit controls for words and characters.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can see over/under by exact assignment, form, or platform limit.

proposed_features.suggestion: Align the word-count formula with Reading Time Calculator or clearly label the difference.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users avoid small count discrepancies between related Kefiw tools.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add optional custom WPM settings directly on the Word Counter page.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Script and article users can tune timing without switching pages.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add a compact copy-stats button.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can paste counts into editor notes, assignments, or content QA checklists.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add a warning for text that appears to lack spaces between words.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users with CJK or Thai text get a clear explanation before relying on word count.

user_scenarios: A writer needs to know whether a profile line fits a character limit.
A puzzle solver wants to spot repeated letters in a clue or rack.
A teacher wants a simple frequency example for students.
A developer or content editor wants a quick count without opening a spreadsheet.
tips: Use the frequency list for quick patterns, not advanced statistical analysis.
Use Word Counter when you need words, sentences, paragraphs, and timing.
For text with accents or emoji, rely more on the raw character totals than the letter buckets.
Normalize case before comparing samples, or remember that this frequency list already lowercases letters.
Pair with Case Converter when the next step is formatting rather than counting.

internal_links.reason: Use when the task is draft length rather than letter frequency.
Use after counting when formatting needs cleanup.
Explains word, character, and letter-count differences.
Useful when text length affects timing.

schema_suggestions.notes: Describe the browser-based utility, input/output, and free access without implying server processing.
Use the enhanced FAQ questions and answers.
Use concise how-to steps when the page template supports it.
Match the Word Tools or Calculators cluster path.

existing_features: Counts characters with and without spaces.
Reports ASCII letter and digit frequency sorted by count.
Summarizes vowels, consonants, digits, and punctuation.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add Unicode-aware grapheme counting.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Emoji, accents, and combined characters match user expectations more closely.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add an ASCII vs Unicode breakdown.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can see why accented names or emoji do not appear in the letter-frequency list.

proposed_features.suggestion: Show frequency percentages in addition to raw counts.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Frequency analysis becomes easier when comparing two samples of different lengths.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add copy/export options for the frequency table.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Teachers, puzzle solvers, and editors can move results into notes or spreadsheets.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add optional charts for top letters.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Visual learners can spot dominant letters faster.

user_scenarios: A blogger pastes a title from notes and wants a simple Title Case version.
A developer converts a UI label into a variable-like name.
A content editor lowercases tags before deduping a list.
A student fixes accidental caps lock before submitting text.
tips: Use lowercase before Remove Duplicate Lines when case-insensitive dedupe is the goal.
Use Title Case for quick headings, then manually fix small words if a style guide matters.
Use snake_case or kebab-case only when punctuation loss is acceptable.
Check the length delta when converting to identifier formats.
Pair with Sort Lines after normalizing a list’s case.

internal_links.reason: Use after case conversion for list cleanup.
Use when lowercase conversion makes exact duplicates easier to catch.
Useful when capitalization is part of a draft cleanup.
Shows where case conversion fits in multi-step cleanup.

schema_suggestions.notes: Describe the browser-based utility, input/output, and free access without implying server processing.
Use the enhanced FAQ questions and answers.
Use concise how-to steps when the page template supports it.
Match the Word Tools or Calculators cluster path.

existing_features: Eight case styles with a copyable output area.
Multi-line input support.
Length-change summary after conversion.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add style-guide title case modes for AP, Chicago, APA, and MLA.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Writers can format headlines with fewer manual corrections.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add acronym and proper-noun preservation options.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Brand names, initialisms, and names are less likely to be damaged by conversion.

proposed_features.suggestion: Use Unicode-aware tokenization for accented letters.
proposed_features.user_benefit: International names and words survive identifier conversion more predictably.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add a visual diff or highlight for changed characters.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can spot removed punctuation, hyphens, or accents before copying.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add quick presets for tags, filenames, and headings.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Beginners can choose a goal rather than learning every case name first.

user_scenarios: A puzzle maker needs a clue written backwards.
A user pastes a chronological list and wants newest-first order.
A teacher demonstrates character reversal.
A writer experiments with word order for a line or prompt.
tips: Use Lines mode for lists and logs, not Characters mode.
Use Words mode when the sentence should remain readable word-by-word.
Remove or review complex emoji before character reversal.
For palindrome checks, remember that punctuation is not fully ignored.
Pair with Sort Lines when list order needs more than a simple flip.

internal_links.reason: Useful for creative formatting after reversal.
Use for ordered list cleanup.
Useful when line reversal is part of list cleanup.
Shows where reversal fits among list operations.

schema_suggestions.notes: Describe the browser-based utility, input/output, and free access without implying server processing.
Use the enhanced FAQ questions and answers.
Use concise how-to steps when the page template supports it.
Match the Word Tools or Calculators cluster path.

existing_features: Reverses by characters, words, or lines.
Preserves whitespace groups in Words mode.
Shows a palindrome hint in Characters mode.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add grapheme-cluster reversal with Intl.Segmenter fallback logic.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Emoji, accents, flags, and combined characters are less likely to break.

proposed_features.suggestion: Normalize CRLF line endings before Lines mode output.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Technical and Windows-origin text reverses with fewer hidden-character surprises.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add Reverse Each Word mode.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can flip letters inside words without changing sentence word order.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add optional punctuation-insensitive palindrome detection.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Classic phrase palindromes become easier to check.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add a clearly labeled mirror-glyph generator as a separate mode.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users searching for visual mirror text get the correct effect without confusing it with reversal.

user_scenarios: A content editor alphabetizes tags before publication.
A teacher sorts vocabulary words by length.
A developer reviews a pasted list of labels.
A user sorts a numbered plain-text list before deduping.
tips: Use case-insensitive alpha sort for most everyday lists.
Use length descending when you want to find outliers or overly long items.
Use Remove Duplicate Lines after sorting if the duplicate stat matters.
Trim or inspect whitespace before length sorting if visible length is important.
For file names with numbers, watch for natural-sort edge cases until that feature exists.

internal_links.reason: Most list cleanup workflows combine sorting and dedupe.
Use when flipping order is enough and sorting is not needed.
Useful when capitalization should not affect list review.
Explains which sort mode fits each task.

Use the enhanced FAQ questions and answers.

existing_features: Six sort modes: alpha asc/desc, length asc/desc, numeric asc/desc.
Case-sensitive toggle for alpha sorting and duplicate counts.
Duplicate and unique counts after sorting.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add true natural/version sorting.
proposed_features.user_benefit: File names and labels such as item-2 and item-10 sort in human-friendly order.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add trim and remove-empty-line toggles.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can prepare messy pasted lists without manual cleanup first.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add locale selection or explain current locale in the UI.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users with accented text understand why order differs across environments.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add sort-and-dedupe as an explicit optional output mode.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Common cleanup workflows take fewer copy-paste steps while staying transparent.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add a before/after preview for the first changed lines.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can see whether the selected mode did what they expected.

user_scenarios: A marketer pastes tags from several tools and needs unique entries.
A student combines vocabulary lists and removes repeats.
A site owner cleans duplicate URLs before checking counts.
A developer dedupes simple line-based config snippets.
tips: Normalize case before dedupe when capitalization should not matter.
Dedupe last after any planned case or whitespace cleanup.
Preserve order when the first occurrence carries context.
Sort unique output only when alphabetical order is more useful than original order.
Use Word Counter or line stats afterward to verify the cleanup.

internal_links.reason: Useful workaround for case-insensitive dedupe.

internal_links.anchor_text: sort lines before or after dedupe
internal_links.reason: Related list-cleanup task.
Use to confirm how much text changed.
Guide explains when dedupe helps and when it hides useful frequency.

existing_features: Removes duplicate lines by exact line match after trailing-whitespace trim.
Keeps the first occurrence.
Preserve-order toggle, with sorted unique output when disabled.
Reports input, output, removed, and unique-rate stats.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add a case-insensitive matching toggle.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can dedupe “Apple,” “apple,” and “APPLE” without a separate case-conversion step.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add trim-leading and trim-all-whitespace options.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Copied tables and indented lists produce fewer missed duplicates.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add show removed duplicates with counts and line numbers.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can audit what changed before trusting the cleaned list.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add Unicode normalization before matching as an option.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Visually identical accented strings are less likely to remain as separate lines.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add keep-first vs keep-last mode.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users can choose whether earliest or latest data wins.

user_scenarios: A writer wants a “5 min read” target.
A presenter needs a draft to fit a time slot.
A teacher estimates how long an assignment takes to read.
A podcaster checks whether a script section is too long.
tips: Use a slower speaking WPM for formal talks or dense material.
Add a buffer for pauses, slide transitions, and emphasis.
Use Word Counter first when you also need characters, sentences, or paragraphs.
Rehearse spoken scripts after using the estimate.
Keep WPM consistent when comparing multiple drafts.

internal_links.reason: Use when words, characters, sentences, and paragraphs matter together.
Useful when text also has strict character limits.

internal_links.reason: Explains when timing, words, or characters matter most.
Helpful when timing is part of a larger editing workflow.

proposed_features.suggestion: Align the word-count formula with Word Counter or display a formula note.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users get consistent counts across linked Kefiw tools.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add presets for blog post, speech, narration, and classroom reading.
proposed_features.user_benefit: Users choose a real task and get sensible WPM starting points.

proposed_features.suggestion: Add optional pause/buffer controls for speaking time.
